title: SpaceX History Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: SpaceX formally entered the public markets process on April 1, 2026, filing a confidential IPO registration statement with the SEC — codenamed 'Project Apex' — targeting a ~$75 billion raise at a ~$1.75 trillion valuation in what would be the largest IPO in U.S. history, with a June 2026 Nasdaq listing and Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs as lead underwriters. Starship IFT-12, the debut of the V3 architecture with Raptor 3 engines, slipped from its late-April target to approximately May 2026 as Booster 19 awaited reinstallation of its remaining 23 engines before a full 33-engine static fire. The Starlink 17-35 rideshare mission (25 satellites, B1103 debut flight) suffered four consecutive scrubs through April 5 and is now targeting April 6 from Vandenberg.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: Starship IFT-12 Timeline Slips from Late April to May 2026 — Booster 19 Awaits 33-Engine Static Fire. SpaceX scrubbed the Starlink Group 17-35 launch attempt from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base on April 2, 2026, within the 4:03–8:03 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: SpaceX closed March 2026 with a historic double-launch day on March 30: Transporter-16 orbited 119 payloads from Vandenberg (booster B1093, 12th flight), crossing the 1,600-total-rideshare-payload milestone, while Starlink 10-44 from Cape Canaveral saw booster B1067 set a world record with its 34th flight — the most flights ever by a single orbital rocket stage. The two March 30 launches pushed SpaceX's 2026 total to 40 missions and all-time booster recoveries to 593. Starship IFT-12 preparations advanced with Ship 39 completing cryoloading preflight objectives at Starbase; the FAA license window runs April 5–October 5, 2026, with a late-April target pending a full Booster 19 static fire. The SpaceX IPO process is advancing, with investor meetings scheduled for April and Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs confirmed as lead underwriters.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: SpaceX's Starship Ship 39 — the first V3 Starship upper stage — completed multiple cryogenic propellant loading preflight test objectives at Starbase, Boca Chica, on March 31, 2026.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Transporter-16, its 16th dedicated rideshare mission, at 4:02 a.m. SpaceX launched the Starlink Group 10-44 mission at 5:15 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched its 38th mission of 2026 on March 26 with Starlink 17-17 from Vandenberg, booster B1081's 23rd flight, achieving its 591st total booster recovery (186th on OCISLY). The week's biggest corporate news came from Gwynne Shotwell's TIME magazine cover story (March 26), which revealed SpaceX filed an FCC application to license up to 1 million AI-powered orbital data center satellites, and that SpaceX is scheduling investor briefings for April ahead of an IPO targeting up to $75 billion — with the combined SpaceX+xAI entity valued at $1.25–1.75 trillion. The Transporter-16 rideshare mission (119 payloads) was scrubbed twice on March 23 and 25 due to winds and a boat in the keep-out zone. Booster B1067's record-setting 34th flight on Starlink 10-44 was delayed multiple times, slipping to late March. KPIs, claims, and political profiles were updated to reflect the xAI merger, IPO developments, and Shotwell's new corporate role.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: SpaceX's planned launch of Starlink Group 10-44 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station was delayed from its targeted March 27 window.

title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: SpaceX crossed a landmark milestone on March 16–17, 2026, when a Falcon 9 Starlink Group 17-24 mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base pushed the active Starlink constellation past 10,000 satellites simultaneously in orbit — less than seven years after the first Starlink launch and now representing roughly two-thirds of all active satellites worldwide. SpaceX continued its extraordinary 2026 launch cadence with Falcon 9 missions on March 19 (29 satellites from Cape Canaveral), March 20 (25 satellites from Vandenberg, booster B1100's 4th flight), and March 22 (Starlink 10-62, booster B1078's record 27th flight from Cape Canaveral). Meanwhile, Starship Flight 12 — the debut of the upgraded V3 configuration with Raptor 3 engines — was pushed from a March target to approximately April 2026 following a booster anomaly during pressure testing and subsequent FAA licensing for the April 5–October 5, 2026 window.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: SpaceX's second launch attempt for the Transporter-16 rideshare mission was scrubbed when a boat entered the keep-out zone around SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: SpaceX's first launch attempt for the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base was scrubbed due to high upper-level winds.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched the Starlink 10-62 mission on a Falcon 9 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, deploying 29 Starlink satellites.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched its 30th Starlink batch of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 from Space Launch Complex 4 East at Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Mar 19, 2026
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket lifted off from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station at 10:20 a.m.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 16, 2026
summary: SpaceX's Starlink Group 17-24 mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 10:19 p.m.
